profile.name: Alessandro Nocentini
profile.headline: Automation and Control Engineer | AI Research Engineer

paragraphs: MSc graduate in Automation and Control Engineering from Politecnico di Milano. I'm a highly motivated individual with a passion for tackling challenges and applying learned concepts in real-world applications. During my Bachelor's and Master's degrees, I developed a strong interest in Robotics and Machine Learning.

My distinct traits are self-improvement and multi-disciplinarity. I like undertaking projects to challenge my capabilities and expand my skillset, and this led me to develop a versatile and variegated range of skills (both technical and non-technical) that spans across different fields.
I am constantly ready to face new challenges, confident of my problem solving skills and detail-oriented mindset, aiming to meet and even exceed other people's expectations.
On my free time I am an active person, who loves to practice different sports, and a creative mind, with the passion for traditional and digital art forms.

work.company: AIRIC | Artificial Intelligence Research and Innovation Center - Politecnico di Milano
work.description: Research and development of AI solutions to solve real-world problems of companies of various industrial sectors.

work.company: Department of Mechanical Engineering - Politecnico di Milano
work.domain: Prognostics & Health Monitoring (PHM), Avionics Embedded Systems
work.description: Research and development of Health Monitoring methods in avionic systems, analyzing data packet retransmissions under extreme thermal and mechanical stress. In collaboration with Leonardo S.p.A., responsible for full-cycle development including laboratory environment configuration, embedded software module design for electronic boards, rigorous environmental testing (high temperature and vibration), and the implementation of anomaly detection algorithms.

education.thesis:
**A User-Friendly Tool for the Verification of Protection Systems in LV Distribution Grids**
In partnership with ABB, developed a user-friendly tool for the formal verification of protection systems in LV distribution grids. This involved creating a custom notation (Simulink library) for easy graphical grid modeling and implementing an intuitive application (MATLAB app) to control the process. Designed and implemented supplementary features to enhance the user experience, validated through summative usability testing.
education.description: Control of Industrial Robots, Data Driven Control System Design, Advanced and Multivariable Control, Software Engineering, Machine Learning, Reinforcement Learning, Computer Aided Manufacturing
Process Control, Electrical Machines and Drives, Fundamentals of Robotics, Mechanical Systems Modeling
Placeholder education entry to demonstrate the timeline layout with multiple items.

projects.name: Automation and Control Laboratory
projects.shortDescription: Design, simulation and testing of control techniques on a Quanser Torsion System.
projects.longDescription: Performed experiments on a Quanser Multi-dof Torsion System to acquire data and model the plant, estimating the uncertain parameters. Designed, simulated and tested different control techniques (from the simplest model-based PID controllers, to the advanced data-driven approaches like VRFT and Self Tuning) through MATLAB and Simulink softwares.
